rationale: The page contains a detailed summary table of various missiles (BrahMos, Astra, Pralay, Akash Prime, ULPGM-V3, Rampage) and a specific section on Scramjet Engine testing by DRDO. This technical information is highly relevant for competitive examinations.
cleanedText:
6.2.2. OTHER MISSILES IN NEWS
| Missile | Key Detail/Features |
| :--- | :--- |
| BrahMos Missile | • Jointly developed by India and Russia. • A long-range supersonic cruise missile that can be launched from land, sea and air. • Two-stage system: 1st stage (Solid propellant booster): Gives the initial supersonic speed; 2nd stage (Liquid ramjet): Takes the missile closer to 3 Mach speed in cruise phase. • Range: Up to 290 km. • Special Capabilities: Fire and Forget technology; Shorter flight times lead to lower target dispersion and quicker engagement. |
| Astra Missile | • Indigenous Beyond Visual Range Air-to-Air missile (BVRAAM). • Designed to engage and destroy highly manoeuvring supersonic aircraft. • Developed by: DRDO. • The missile has all weather day and night capability. • Equipped with state-of-the-art guidance and navigation systems. • Range: Exceeds 100 kms. |
| Pralay missile | • A surface-to-surface short range solid propellant quasi-ballistic missile. • Can fly at hypersonic speed. • Developed indigenously by: Hyderabad-based DRDO facility RCI in collaboration with other facilities. • Strike Range: From 150 to 500 km. |
| Akash Prime | • Developed by DRDO. • Hard and rugged version of the indigenous Akash SAM system. • Designed to operate in very extreme weather in cold areas, high altitude (above 4500 meter) and low-oxygen environments. |
| ULPGM-V3 | • ULPGM-V3 (UAV Launched Precision Guided Missile (ULPGM)-V3) is developed by DRDO. • Equipped with a high definition dual-channel seeker that can strike a wide variety of targets. • Significance: Can be fired in plain and high-altitude areas. Day-and-night capability and two-way data link to support post-launch target/aim-point update. |
| Rampage Missiles | • Long range, air to ground precise attack missile. • Travel at supersonic speeds, making it difficult to detect. • Developed by: Israel Aerospace Industries. |
6.3. SCRAMJET ENGINE
Why in the News?
Defence Research & Development Laboratory (DRDL), a laboratory of DRDO, successfully completes 1000+ seconds Active Cooled Scramjet Subscale Combustor ground testing.
More on the News
• This marks crucial milestone in developing Hypersonic Weapon Technology.
